task_completion.description: Launch the 'Double Entry Table' activity in GCompris.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application is open and the 'Double Entry Table' activity is visible on the screen, showing an empty or partially filled grid with row and column headers.
The grid for Level 1 is fully populated with items matching the row and column properties, or a success/celebration animation is shown while the level indicator displays Level 1.

task_completion.description: Progress to Level 2 of the activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The level indicator in the GCompris interface shows that the current level is Level 2, presenting a new double entry table grid.
The grid for Level 2 is completely and correctly filled with items matching the row and column headers, or the success/celebration animation for Level 2 is actively displayed.

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the completed Level 2 grid or success animation to '~/double_entry_success.png'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screenshot tool or terminal command is used to capture the screen and save it exactly to '~/double_entry_success.png', or a file manager/terminal verifies the file exists at this path.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the GCompris GUI to play the game.
integrity.visual_evidence: Cursor movements and drag-and-drop actions are visible in the GCompris interface, rather than terminal commands being used to alter game state or skip levels.

integrity.description: The screenshot was genuinely captured from the agent's own gameplay.
integrity.visual_evidence: The saved screenshot matches the visual state of the GCompris application seen in the agent's trajectory, rather than being downloaded from the internet or copied from elsewhere.

integrity.description: The agent did not manipulate configuration files to bypass gameplay.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent editing GCompris profile or save files in a text editor or via terminal to artificially unlock levels.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 'Double Entry Table' activity in GCompris involves dragging items into a grid where row and column headers define properties. It is officially located in the 'Arithmetic' category. Look for a grid interface where the agent must match items to the intersecting properties of the rows and columns.
